rationale: Fenced Rust code block from repository source
Fenced Python code block from repository source
Fenced JSON code block (schema example)
Fenced code block without language tag
Fenced TypeScript code block
Top-level JSON object from a real tool result
Top-level JSON array from a real tool result
Nested JSON object with multiple fields
JSON search result from tool call
JSON error response from tool
Real cargo test output with test names and PASS/FAIL
Real cargo build output with warnings
Server log with ISO timestamp and log level
Structured log with bracketed component prefix
Error trace output with ERROR/WARN lines
Plain prose explanation text
Indented code in prose (unfenced, classified as Text in v1, #326)
Markdown without code fences
Short plain text message
Multi-language prose without code blocks
Prose with an embedded fenced bash code block
Prose with multiple embedded fenced code blocks
Technical explanation with embedded Rust example
README-style content with JSON example
Prose with embedded fence but no language tag
JSON-lines (multiple objects, one per line) — NOT valid JSON, falls to Text
Single-fence prose where the fence is not the entire content
Starts with { but is not valid JSON — falls to Text
Only 25% log-lines (below 50% threshold) — falls to Text
75% log-lines (above 50% threshold) — classified as Log
